Keith Anwar repaired trains wrote plays with equal pride.
maintenance technician with Chicago Transit Authority nearly years, Anwar's script "Kabulitis," Afghan term dysentery other intestinal illnesses, awarded 2010 Dionysos Chicago Polarity Ensemble Theatre's Festival Plays June.
Last month, staged reading Anwar's script performed theater, fully staged play being considered 2011-12 theater season.
Anwar, died liver cancer Monday, July Park home, family said.
Anwar's father native Afghanistan with Brooklyn-born wife took part ill-fated attempt promote secularism modern thinking mid-20th century Afghanistan.
Afghanistan parents received numerous death threats, part because mother refused yield demands wear veil stance husband supported eventually were forced leave country.
"Kabulitis," touching drama based mother's experiences, tells story elderly American woman early stages Alzheimer's disease haunted memories Afghanistan.
parents returned York, where Anwar born raised.
received bachelor's degree history 1974 from Brandeis University, near Boston, then moved Chicago began working Inland Steel East Chicago.
studied labor movement college then found himself experiencing firsthand hard realities involving workers' rights," said wife years, Connie Pfiffner.
1979, member United Steelworkers America Local 1010, Anwar fired refusal cross picket line another USWA local that plant, family said.
campaign Anwar reinstated generated widespread support among steelworkers Chicago-Gary area.
That support critical obtaining favorable decision grievance National Labor Relations Board.
board's decision overturned 1983 federal appeals court that backed company, Anwar's family said.
"Keith acted defense tradition that built industrial unions this country: Picket lines mean don't cross," said brother, Bruce.
Anwar worked other Chicago-area manufacturing plants before landing maintenance technician with nearly years ago.
"Keith took great pride being what called 'train repair man,'" wife said.
Several years ago, Anwar joined Park Writers Group, Chicago Dramatists Dramatists Guild.
began writing series short plays, many which were given staged readings area theaters.
currently browsing Polarity Ensemble Theatre Blog blog archives July, 2010.

total, each well brings hundreds thousands millions gallons wastewater, which, Heinberg says, "carries with only secret cocktail chemicals added that accomplish mission, also highly corrosive salts, carcinogenic benzene, radioactive elements like cesium uranium, leached from rock strata miles underground."

"What's fracker with this toxic wastewater?"
asks Heinberg.
Frackers Pennsylvania send York among other states (like Ohio).
According Ellen Weininger Grassroots Environmental Education, data released Pennsylvania Department Environmental Protection reveal that past years alone, more than 300,000 tons drilling waste including wastewater drill cuttings have been accepted from fracking operations Pennsylvania into York landfills.
Weininger notes that leachate from landfills then accepted wastewater treatment plants, none which designed process radioactive materials other drilling waste contaminants.
"The fact don't know what's waste coming into state, we're sufficiently regulating treatment those wastes, cannot certain that treatment plants adequately protecting lakes, rivers streams," says Senator Tkaczyk.
fact that landfills sewage treatment facilities York accepting this waste before Cuomo Administration completes review decides whether open state high volume horizontal fracking influenced Tkacyzk's decision introduce legislation, which ultimately reach floor vote.
makes sense that would accept hazardous wastes from other states while working determine environmental impact fracking would have York," says Senator Tkacyzk.
worth noting that York only state that importing fracking waste from other states while under moratorium banning high volume horizontal drilling inside state.
"With little action state legislature concerned immediate threat public health environment posed hazardous, radioactive drilling waste," says Weininger, "several York counties have banned sale fracking waste, processing wastewater treatment plants application roads including de-icing dust control purposes."
Nassau (where live) Suffolk Counties Long Island poised follow lead other York Counties enact more comprehensive legislation prohibiting drilling waste.

Over years, have read every book magazine article could hands that concerned drums.
have spent countless hours perusing drum catalogs literature, well repairing restoring hundreds vintage drums.
